chunks.title: Eliphaz Rebukes Job's Wisdom
chunks.reason: Eliphaz opens his speech by questioning the value of Job's words and asserting that Job's own mouth condemns him.

chunks.title: The Humility of Man Before God
chunks.reason: Eliphaz argues that Job lacks divine wisdom and questions the purity of man compared to God's holy ones.

chunks.title: The Fate of the Wicked Man
chunks.reason: Eliphaz recounts traditional wisdom describing the constant pain and ultimate destruction awaiting the wicked.
